description: A candid photograph (no graphic design or overlay) of an opened cardboard care-package box filled with snacks and drinks (a Celsius energy drink carton, individually bagged candy, granola/snack bars, fruit snacks, etc.). Resting on top is a handwritten thank-you note in blue ink that reads approximately: 'Thank You doesn't express how thankful our family is for your service. You face unbelievable obstacles every day. Know there are so many American patriots that are praying for you without ceasing! God Bless You and Protect You! The [redacted] family of Omaha'. The family surname is blurred/redacted for privacy. This is a documentary photo of a supporter's donation to ICE/law-enforcement officers in Minneapolis, not an agency-designed graphic; the only text is the handwritten note itself. Intent: humanize officers and showcase grassroots public support amid the tweet's narrative of officers enduring assaults and threats. Target audience: ICE personnel and sympathetic public.
notable_text: Thank You doesn't express how thankful our family is for your service... Know there are so many American patriots that are praying for you without ceasing! God Bless You and Protect You! The [redacted] family of Omaha
